name: rote-onboard
description: rote skills — first-run setup, adapter create (dry-run-first), adapter config, registry (share + usage), and update.

interface.shortDescription: Setup, adapter creation (dry-run-first), configuration, registry sharing, and updates for rote.
interface.longDescription: A suite of conversational skills for rote. rote-setup: install → identity-gated sign-in → fork (stop / powerpack / build an adapter). rote-adapter-create: dry-run-first adapter creation from the 872-spec catalog, a web-found spec, or a local file — analyzes the spec without committing, then pre-fills auth and toolset choices from the analysis. rote-adapter-config: tune an existing adapter (auth, base-url, write guard, sensitivity, capability index, re-auth, GraphQL field filter, policies, subagent). rote-registry: at the moment an adapter is minted or a flow crystallized, check whether it already exists in your orgs, push at a chosen visibility, then surface members and offer invites — plus show usage/quota across all your orgs. rote-update: update the binary and the skills together. Every branch is a clear choice — nothing runs silently.
defaultPrompt: Add an adapter for an API
